summary: Verified 4 user-reported production issues are fixed via code verification tests. All 14 tests passed. (1) Recommendation enforcement: Each AI model's recommendation is now forced to match projectedValue vs line BEFORE consensus calculation in both predict.py and basketball_predict.py. (2) Position comparison specific position filter: fetch_position_comparison now accepts target_specific_pos parameter and filters out players whose cached specificPosition doesn't match. (3) Team dedup: Position comparison now limits to max 1 player per team (prevents 4 Flamengo players). (4) Fixture limit: Position comparison now searches up to 10 fixtures (increased from 5).
critical_code_review_comments: All 4 user-reported issues verified fixed in code:

1. RECOMMENDATION ENFORCEMENT: Lines 1379-1380 in predict.py and 871-872 in basketball_predict.py now enforce r['recommendation'] = 'over' if pv > req.line else 'under' for EACH model before consensus. Also enforced after averaging at lines 1399-1400 and 885-886.

2. SPECIFIC POSITION FILTER: fetch_position_comparison at line 325 now accepts target_specific_pos parameter. Lines 402-404 filter out players where spec_pos != target_specific_pos. Call site at line 1121 passes target_specific_pos=specific_position.
3. TEAM DEDUP: Lines 431-442 implement max 1 player per team using seen_teams dict with check 'seen_teams.get(team, 0) >= 1'.
4. FIXTURE LIMIT: Function signature at line 325 has limit=10 (not 5). Line 424 uses opp_fixtures[:limit] for task creation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 37 verified 4 specific user-reported production issues are fixed: (1) Recommendation enforcement ensures projectedValue vs line determines recommendation for each AI model BEFORE consensus - eliminates 'GP says OVER with 58.2 when line is 60.5' contradiction. (2) Position comparison now filters by specific position (CB vs LB) - fixes Alex Sandro showing as CB when he's LB. (3) Max 1 player per team in position comparison - fixes 4 Flamengo players appearing. (4) Fixture limit increased to 10 for more diverse data. All tests pass via code verification.
